title: My Creative Journey
subtitle: Beyond the Grid: How a Campus Problem Launched My Design Career
excerpt:
In September 2023, I walked onto campus as a fresh first-year student and immediately noticed a glaring problem. The communication posters for our student clubs were cluttered, chaotic, and completely failing to connect. Having spent hours analyzing world-class typography and layouts on Pinterest, I knew the visual standards on campus had to change.Driven by raw curiosity—and the practical need to earn some pocket money—I decided to bridge that gap. 
    The real turning point came when I connected with Peterson Gachii in the Christian Union. Peterson became a massive influence on my journey, opening my eyes to what professional design actually looked like. By picking his brain, reverse-engineered workflows, and diving into endless nights of independent research, I treated every single club announcement as a high-stakes design brief. I constantly refined my eye for balance, hierarchy, and color theory.What started as a student hustle quickly exploded into a deep obsession with visual strategy. Today, as a third-year student, I have evolved those early campus experiments into a professional graphic design practice. I no longer just make posters look pretty; I build cohesive, intentional visual systems that demand attention and deliver results.

title: Design Thinking
subtitle: Why Boring Minimalism is Officially Dead
excerpt:
The Shift:For the past ten years, corporate graphic design felt incredibly safe and predictable. Every brand used the exact same clean geometric fonts, massive white spaces, and flat, muted colors. It was the peak of boredom minimalism. But as we look at the current creative landscape, the design world is finally fighting back.
    The Trend:We are currently witnessing a massive rise in Expressive Maximalism and Retro-Futurism. Brands are completely abandoning safe layouts in favor of:Heavy, distorted typography that pushes the boundaries of legibility.Gritty, analog textures that make digital screens feel tactile and human.Clashing, high-contrast color combinations inspired by early 90s cyber aesthetics.The Takeaway:In a digital world completely flooded with content, looking clean is no longer enough to get noticed. The future of graphic design belongs to brave visuals that value raw personality over perfect, safe neatness.
